title: SoFi Q1 2026: Member Growth and Cross-Sell Acceleration
text: SoFi added 1.1 million new members in Q1 2026, bringing its total membership to 14.7 million—a 35% year-over-year increase. The company also added 1.8 million new products, reaching 22.2 million total products, representing 39% growth annually. Approximately 43% of newly opened products came from existing members, up from 36% a year earlier. This acceleration demonstrates customers are expanding their relationships within the platform over time. CEO Anthony Noto emphasized the strategic importance: The numbers in the quarter clearly demonstrates the effectiveness of our Everything Financial Services app strategy and the company's ability to build multi-product relationships that will drive a higher lifetime value. SoFi reported $1.1 billion in quarterly revenue, up 41% year-over-year. The revenue composition is shifting beyond lending: Net interest income approximately $690 million, Fees (interchange, brokerage, technology, loan platforms) approximately $390 million. The company originated $12.2 billion in loans during the quarter, its highest level to date, including $8.3 billion in personal loans reflecting demand for credit card refinancing. Despite strong growth metrics, investors sent the stock down 12% following earnings, primarily due to a 27% decline in technology platform revenue tied to a major client transition.
